focus: correctness — deterministic hub scoring and outcome semantics

findingDetails.title: Explicit Figma render/export scenario produces no Figma intent
findingDetails.evidence: The deterministic benchmark uses exact lowercase substring matching. The declared prompt “Render this component in Figma and export the design tokens” contains none of the Figma phrases as contiguous substrings, so replay returns intents=[] and the Chrome default resource.
findingDetails.impact: The central figma-transport routing question fails on the committed positive scenario and contradicts the advisor trigger example.
findingDetails.recommendation: Route through a separate remediation/ADR amendment; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Hub router vocabulary, committed hub-routing scenario, and the shipped deterministic replay path were read directly.

findingDetails.title: Chrome packet is unconditionally loaded on every non-Chrome route
findingDetails.evidence: routerPolicy.defaultResource points at mcp-chrome-devtools/SKILL.md, while assembleResources always unions every defaultResource before selected-mode resources. Replay therefore loads Chrome alongside ClickUp, Aside, Refero, and Mobbin, contrary to their expected_resources and single-mode statements.
findingDetails.impact: Correct mode telemetry still receives the wrong sibling packet contract, contaminating execution context and violating single-mode resource isolation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat as a separate router-policy remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The policy field and benchmark resource assembly consumer were inspected at their exact source lines.

findingDetails.title: Shared hub-identity vocabulary defeats the defer contract
findingDetails.evidence: Every mode references hub-identity at weight 4, and that class includes “mcp tool bridge”. MT-004 uses that exact phrase, so all six modes tie at score 4 and are selected instead of producing no dominant signal/defer.
findingDetails.impact: A deliberately ambiguous request expands to all six packets even though hub SKILL.md and MT-004 require disambiguation.
findingDetails.recommendation: Address in a separate router-policy remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All six signal class lists, the shared keyword class, the scenario, and selection algorithm were inspected.

findingDetails.title: Five of six blind hub holdouts have zero lexical recall
findingDetails.evidence: Deterministic replay routes only MT-H01. MT-H02/H03/H04/H05/H06 each score no mode because their natural-language paraphrases are absent from the exact-substring vocabulary, then inherit the Chrome default resource.
findingDetails.impact: The committed corpus claims cross-surface natural-language routing that the shipped replay cannot reproduce; Aside, ClickUp, Figma, Refero, and Mobbin each lose their blind holdout.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use the benchmark result as input to a separate vocabulary amendment; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All six holdout prompts were replayed through the shipped router and compared to their frontmatter expectations.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Six-mode router with shared vocabularies, default/defer semantics, and cross-hub design pairing is a multi-axis routing contract.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the declarative producer, registry projection, gold corpus, and deterministic consumer for routing outcomes.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The committed Figma render/export prompt selects mcp-figma.

searchActions.result: No mode scored; Chrome default resource returned.

searchLedger.rationale: Observed deterministic output contradicts the expected intent.

searchLedger.invariant: A single non-Chrome mode route loads only that mode packet.

searchActions.result: Chrome resource is added before every selected mode resource.

searchLedger.rationale: Default resource behaves as unconditional preamble, not fallback.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Bare hub identity produces no dominant mode and defers.

searchActions.result: All six modes tied at score 4.

searchLedger.rationale: Shared hub identity is a strong per-mode signal in the replay.

searchLedger.hypothesis: All six natural-language holdouts retain their intended mode boundary.

searchActions.result: Only MT-H01 scored its intended mode; the other five scored no mode.

searchLedger.rationale: The committed exact-substring vocabulary does not contain the blind paraphrases.

focus: security — transport boundaries, vocabulary collisions, and design ownership

findingDetails.title: Generic screen-examples phrase loads both Refero and Mobbin
findingDetails.evidence: The exact phrase screen examples exists in both design-reference-research and mobbin-aliases. Replay of an unqualified signup-flow request selects both remote transports and returns both packets, even though neither provider was named.
findingDetails.impact: Generic design research over-dispatches two external provider contracts instead of preserving provider choice or design-hub judgment.
findingDetails.recommendation: Address as a separate vocabulary/route-policy remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The duplicated producer vocabulary, deterministic selection algorithm, concrete replay, and both packet exclusions were inspected.

findingDetails.title: Figma transport is marked workspace-read-only despite local export writes
findingDetails.evidence: Registry toolSurface sets mutatesWorkspace false. The Figma packet assigns CLI export operations to allowed Bash and states that local exports write files and require an explicit output path.
findingDetails.impact: Consumers can treat a locally mutating transport as non-mutating, weakening permission, preview, or audit decisions derived from registry metadata.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile in a separate registry/packet contract remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The registry flag and packet's explicit local-write exception were traced through the allowed Bash surface.

findingDetails.title: Figma authoring path lacks mandatory sk-design pairing
findingDetails.evidence: Figma exposes document-mutating author/modify and design-token paths, while both of its sk-design mandates are scoped only to reads/exports that feed a design decision. Hub and sk-design doctrine require transport use after judgment selection.
findingDetails.impact: Figma can be routed to create design artifacts without the designated design-judgment owner, allowing transport capability to absorb taste decisions.
findingDetails.recommendation: Resolve through a separate cross-hub doctrine remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Activation, ALWAYS, integration, parent hub, and sk-design rules were compared directly.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Three overlapping external design transports carry permission and judgment boundaries whose routing collisions can change loaded authority.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These surfaces define transport dispatch, tool permissions, and the cross-hub design-judgment owner.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Generic design-reference language cannot activate multiple named transport providers.

searchActions.queryOrPath: Find screen examples for a signup flow
searchActions.result: Refero and Mobbin tie at score 4 and both packets are returned.

searchLedger.rationale: A duplicated provider vocabulary phrase defeats the explicit-tools bundle boundary.

searchLedger.invariant: A transport classified mutatesWorkspace false has no allowed local file-write path.

searchActions.queryOrPath: toolSurface.mutatesWorkspace to Figma Bash export command class
searchActions.result: Packet explicitly identifies local file writes under allowed Bash exports.

searchLedger.rationale: The metadata trust signal and executable command surface disagree.

searchLedger.invariant: Every design-affecting transport operation is preceded by a sk-design judgment mode.

searchActions.queryOrPath: crossHubPairing doctrine to Figma ALWAYS and integration rules
searchActions.result: Figma pairing text covers only read/export inputs, omitting author/modify and tokens.

searchLedger.rationale: The packet weakens the mandatory parent-hub judgment contract on its most design-affecting path.

focus: traceability — metadata projections, benchmark gates, and phase acceptance

findingDetails.title: Baseline benchmark passes seven route-contract violations
findingDetails.evidence: Report verdict is PASS, hubRoute.regressions is zero, and all 13 scenarios are labeled passed while routeGoldRows is zero. Telemetry records MT-004 selecting six modes and MT-003 plus five holdouts selecting no mode.
findingDetails.impact: The deterministic CI signal certifies broken routing and can release the hub without detecting its positive, defer, or holdout failures.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat as a separate benchmark-schema/gate remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Verdict, gate counters, route-gold counters, all 13 rows, and scenario frontmatter expectations were compared.

findingDetails.title: Derived graph intent projection omits three added modes
findingDetails.evidence: Root intent_signals and derived entities cover all six modes, but derived.intent_signals lists only Chrome, ClickUp, and Figma vocabulary. Edge contexts and causal summary likewise narrate only three modes.
findingDetails.impact: Graph consumers receive inconsistent routing inventories depending on which projection they read, reducing discovery for Aside, Refero, and Mobbin.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile through a separate metadata regeneration/remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All root and derived inventory fields plus outward-edge summaries were compared inside the single graph metadata document.

findingDetails.title: Phase acceptance package remains scoped to three modes
findingDetails.evidence: Spec, plan, and tasks repeatedly define a three-mode matrix and do not name Aside, Refero, or Mobbin. The execution note says only a .gitkeep baseline exists although a six-mode baseline report is committed.
findingDetails.impact: The active target cannot demonstrate requirement-to-scenario or task-to-evidence coverage for half of the current hub inventory.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use a separate planning/spec amendment; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Requirements, success criteria, plan axes, tasks, status, execution note, and benchmark paths were compared.

findingDetails.title: Hub playbook index omits three modes and nine scenarios
findingDetails.evidence: The index describes only Chrome, ClickUp, Figma and a four-scenario table, while the directory contains 13 gold files covering Aside, Refero, Mobbin and six holdouts.
findingDetails.impact: Operators using the documented directory under-test the expanded hub and miss its generalization corpus.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh through a separate documentation task; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Index table and related links were compared to the complete hub_routing file inventory.

results.reason: Requirements and success criteria cover three modes while code exposes six.
Tasks are unchecked and committed evidence passes unscored route expectations.
Registry-router keys and aliases align, but the graph derived projection does not.
Index omits expanded surface and benchmark has zero route-gold rows.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A six-mode hub is projected through registry, router, graph, benchmark, playbook, and phase acceptance consumers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the producer and downstream projections that claim inventory and acceptance coverage.

searchLedger.invariant: A routing scenario cannot pass when observed workflowMode violates expected_intent.

searchActions.queryOrPath: 13 report telemetry rows versus scenario expected_intent
searchActions.result: Seven mismatches are labeled passed while routeGoldRows equals zero.

searchLedger.rationale: The designated deterministic gate does not consume route gold.

searchLedger.invariant: Every published graph projection represents the same six-mode inventory.

searchActions.result: Derived intent signals and narrative edges preserve the original three-mode set.

searchLedger.rationale: The single metadata file exposes contradictory inventories.

searchLedger.invariant: The active acceptance packet covers every current router mode and evidence path.

searchActions.result: No added-mode requirement or task exists; all benchmark axes say three modes.

searchLedger.rationale: Half the current inventory has no requirement-to-evidence chain.

searchLedger.invariant: The operator index enumerates the committed routing corpus.

searchActions.queryOrPath: playbook index versus hub_routing file inventory
searchActions.result: Index lists 4 of 13 scenarios and 3 of 6 modes.

searchLedger.rationale: The documented validation entry point hides the expanded corpus.

focus: maintainability — complete six-packet intra-routing recall and replay/runtime parity

findingDetails.title: Four packet blind holdouts miss their declared intent
findingDetails.evidence: CU-H01, CU-H02, MB-H01, and MB-H02 each produce no deterministic intent. The other eight blind packet holdouts select their declared intent.
findingDetails.impact: ClickUp and Mobbin expose no packet-local generalization margin beyond their literal keyword fixtures.
findingDetails.recommendation: Address through a separate packet vocabulary/gold decision; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 12 blind holdouts across the six packet packs were replayed and compared to expected_intent.

findingDetails.title: All six runtime fallbacks violate negative no-resource gold
findingDetails.evidence: Each negative gold declares none/empty. All six runtime pseudocode no-score branches load a default or hardcoded reference, and five also expose a fallback intent label.
findingDetails.impact: The packet-local suppression corpus and documented runtime behavior cannot both pass, obscuring whether out-of-domain packet invocations are rejected or serviced with fallback context.
findingDetails.recommendation: Resolve as a separate suppression-contract decision; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All six negative files and every packet's zero-score runtime branch were traced.

findingDetails.title: Deterministic replay omits runtime no-match behavior
findingDetails.evidence: Generic replay returns no intents on zero scores and derives only declared DEFAULT_RESOURCE/RESOURCE_MAP paths. Runtime routers return fallback labels; ClickUp loads a hardcoded cupt_commands path deliberately absent from DEFAULT_RESOURCE.
findingDetails.impact: The CI replay cannot validate packet runtime fallback intents or resources, so packet suppression and unknown-route results differ by execution path.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use a separate router parity remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: No-score results were compared across replay and all six runtime pseudocode branches.

findingDetails.title: Eleven positive packet scenarios omit always-loaded base gold
findingDetails.evidence: Four Aside, three Chrome, two Mobbin, and two Refero positive/holdout rows omit the packet's documented universal base reference even though replay returns it.
findingDetails.impact: Enabling exact resource-gold scoring will classify declared base behavior as over-routing or mark the authored gold stale.
findingDetails.recommendation: Adjudicate base-resource legitimacy in a separate gold maintenance pass; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 49 expected_resources sets were compared exactly to replay output and classified by default-resource source.

results.reason: Requirements cover three modes while code exposes six.
Tasks are unchecked and committed evidence passes unscored route expectations.
Registry-router align, graph derived projection does not.
Hub index is stale and 20 of 49 packet rows fail at least one authored assertion.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Six independent packet routers expose 49 authored intent/resource contracts and separate benchmark/runtime representations.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These surfaces form the packet-local routing producers, authored gold consumers, and deterministic execution model.

searchLedger.hypothesis: All 12 packet blind holdouts select their declared intent.

searchActions.queryOrPath: 12 holdout prompts through their packet routers
searchActions.result: 8 pass; both ClickUp and both Mobbin holdouts score no intent.

searchLedger.invariant: An out-of-domain negative loads no packet intent or resource.

searchActions.queryOrPath: negative expected none/empty to runtime zero-score branches
searchActions.result: Every runtime branch loads fallback context; five return a fallback intent label.

searchLedger.rationale: The authored suppression contract and runtime behavior disagree in every packet.

searchLedger.invariant: Deterministic replay reproduces runtime intent/resource selection.

searchActions.result: Replay drops runtime fallback intent labels and cannot see ClickUp's hardcoded fallback resource.

searchLedger.rationale: Execution paths produce different observable route contracts.

searchLedger.invariant: Legitimate universal base loads appear in positive scenario gold.

searchActions.result: 11 positive rows omit a documented always-loaded base reference.

searchLedger.rationale: Authored gold and universal loading declarations are not maintained together.

results.summary: Phase requirements cover three modes while the shipped router exposes six.
Tasks are unchecked and the committed benchmark passes unscored route expectations.
The target is a spec folder, not a skill-agent pair.
The target is not an agent runtime surface.
Registry-router keys and aliases align, but the graph derived projection does not.
The index is stale, hub route gold is unscored, and packet recall contracts drift.
